title: Tender Rich Polish Pork Stew
description: A rich and indulgent Polish dish featuring tender pork, yuzu, and Mediterranean-friendly ingredients, slow-cooked to perfection in a gooey stew.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the pork pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the yuzu juice, chicken broth, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot, and stir to combine.
Return the pork to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
